data.title: Wise: Resilient Performance and U.S. Listing Catalyst Support Buy Rating
data.description:
Wise PLC Class A, the Technology sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst today. Analyst Hannes Leitner from Jefferies maintained a Buy rating on the stock and has a p1,299.00 price target.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Hannes Leitner has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that point to resilient operational performance and favorable near‑term catalysts. He notes that key business trends have remained supportive even as macro conditions have been unsettled, indicating that Wise’s growth drivers are intact heading into FQ4’26.

In addition, he underscores that the company’s exposure to geopolitical risks in the Middle East appears contained, limiting downside from that region. He also sees the planned primary listing shift to the U.S. in FQ2 as a structural positive that could enhance Wise’s visibility, liquidity, and investor base, further underpinning his constructive stance on the stock.

In another report released yesterday, TipRanks – OpenAI also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a p999.00 price target.

data.title: Gold Royalty: Cost-Efficient Growth Upside and Attractive Near-Book Valuation Support Buy Rating
data.description:
In a report released today, Tate Sullivan from Maxim Group maintained a Buy rating on Gold Royalty, with a price target of $7.00.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Tate Sullivan has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including Gold Royalty’s stronger-than-expected fourth quarter and improved cost profile. The company delivered EBITDA above his estimates on lower operating and financing costs, moved from a loss to break-even EPS in the quarter, and reported robust full-year revenue growth while ending 2025 with no debt and a solid cash balance.

At the same time, Sullivan highlights a compelling growth outlook driven by management’s 2026 and 2030 production guidance, which implies substantial increases in gold-equivalent output. Even after trimming his revenue assumptions for softer gold prices, he raises his EPS forecasts on improved cost discipline and views the current valuation near book value as attractive relative to his price target, which is based on a higher future book value multiple.

In another report released on March 20, H.C. Wainwright also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a $6.75 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Hold Rating Maintained on Watsco as SRS–Mingledorff’s Deal Tightens M&A Competition but Share-Price Reaction Seen as Excessive
data.description:
William Blair analyst Ryan Merkel has maintained their neutral stance on WSO stock, giving a Hold rating today.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Ryan Merkel has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors surrounding the SRS–Mingledorff’s transaction and its implications for Watsco. He concludes that the sharp share-price decline following SRS Distribution’s purchase of Mingledorff’s is excessive, because Watsco already competes against this asset in overlapping territories and remains Carrier’s dominant distribution partner in those markets.

At the same time, he acknowledges incremental headwinds: Watsco missed the chance to acquire a strategically important Carrier distributor, and SRS now enters HVAC distribution as a financially strong, additional buyer in an already crowded M&A arena. These developments tighten competition for future deals and could modestly constrain Watsco’s growth via acquisitions, which, in Merkel’s view, supports maintaining a neutral Hold stance rather than moving to a more aggressive rating.

Merkel covers the Industrials sector, focusing on stocks such as QXO Inc, Aaon, and Carlisle Companies. According to TipRanks, Merkel has an average return of 2.7% and a 47.83% success rate on recommended stocks.

In another report released today, TipRanks – Anthropic also reiterated a Hold rating on the stock with a $403.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Titan Mining: Buy Rating on Strengthening Zinc Fundamentals and Strategic Kilbourne Graphite Upside
data.description:
Maxim Group analyst Tate Sullivan has reiterated their bullish stance on TI stock, giving a Buy rating on March 20.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Tate Sullivan has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that highlight Titan Mining’s improving fundamentals and attractive valuation. The company’s latest quarter exceeded his expectations as stronger zinc prices and lower operating costs drove better-than-modeled revenue, EBITDA, and free cash flow, even with lower zinc output.

Sullivan also sees meaningful upside from Titan’s Kilbourne graphite project, which has advanced to concentrate shipments and a feasibility study supported by U.S. EXIM financing, positioning the firm to benefit from rising demand for domestic critical minerals. In his view, anticipated investment by U.S. defense and industrial companies in secure graphite and zinc supply chains, together with his discounted cash flow valuation, support a $6.00 12‑month price target and a Buy recommendation.

In another report released on March 20, H.C. Wainwright also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a $6.75 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
